title: Neuroscience PhD Student: 10 Conversations with Evolving Tool Call Summaries
assumptions: Neel is a neuroscience PhD student who is ambitious, analytical, and deadline-aware.
He values deep work, so calendar events are used for reserved work blocks rather than generic reminders.
He is collaborative but sometimes overcommits, so reminders help him follow through on emails, forms, and coordination.
He is health-conscious but inconsistent under stress, so self-care and logistics only get tool calls when they are specific and actionable.
tool_rules: Tools follow this distinction: Reminder = don’t forget; Reminder update/delete = change or remove an existing reminder; Calendar event = reserve time; Calendar event update/delete = change or cancel an existing calendar block; Alarm = urgent prompt at a specific moment; Alarm update/delete = change or remove an urgent prompt; Notes = reference material; Notes update/delete = change or remove saved reference material.

dialogues.speaker: Advisor
dialogues.utterance: Morning, Neel. Have you looked at the sleep-deprivation pilot data closely enough to decide whether we keep the third condition?
dialogues.tool: None

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: Yes. The signal is cleaner in conditions one and two. The third condition adds noise and makes the sample size problem worse.
dialogues.tool: Notes — pilot data interpretation

dialogues.speaker: Advisor
dialogues.utterance: That matches my impression. Can you send me a one-page summary before lab meeting tomorrow?
dialogues.tool: Reminder — send one-page summary before tomorrow’s lab meeting

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: Yes. I want to block a focused hour this afternoon to write it cleanly.
dialogues.tool: Calendar event — focused writing block this afternoon

dialogues.speaker: Advisor
dialogues.utterance: Good. Include the effect size plot, not just the p-values.
dialogues.tool: Notes — include effect size plot in summary

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: Will do. I also think we should say explicitly that the pilot was underpowered.
dialogues.tool: Notes — mention pilot is underpowered

dialogues.speaker: Advisor
dialogues.utterance: Agreed. Better to be honest now than defensive later.
dialogues.tool: None

dialogues.speaker: Advisor
dialogues.utterance: Also, are you still on track to run participants on Thursday?
dialogues.tool: Reminder — confirm Thursday participant run readiness

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: Mostly. I still need to finish the EEG battery check and reprint the consent forms.
dialogues.tool: Reminder — finish EEG battery check and reprint consent forms

dialogues.speaker: Advisor
dialogues.utterance: Please do both today. If something slips, Thursday becomes messy.
dialogues.tool: Reminder — finish setup tasks today

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: Understood. I’ll finish those before dinner.
dialogues.tool: Alarm — 5:00 PM setup cutoff

dialogues.speaker: Advisor
dialogues.utterance: Great. Send me the summary and a short note on whether we drop condition three.
dialogues.tool: Reminder — send recommendation on dropping condition three

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: Got it. Summary, recommendation, battery check, consent forms.
dialogues.tool: Notes — consolidated advisor action list

dialogues.speaker: Advisor
dialogues.utterance: Perfect. Thanks.
dialogues.tool: None

calendar_events: Focused writing block for pilot summary | Time: Today, 3:00–4:00 PM | Location: Office or lab desk | Description: Write one-page summary of sleep-deprivation pilot data for lab meeting. | Priority: High
reminders: Send one-page pilot summary before tomorrow’s lab meeting | Priority: High
Confirm Thursday participant run readiness | Priority: High
Finish EEG battery check | Priority: High
Reprint consent forms | Priority: High
Finish setup tasks today | Priority: High
Send recommendation on dropping condition three | Priority: High
alarms: 5:00 PM — stop and make sure setup tasks are done | Priority: High
notes: Pilot study decisions
Pilot data interpretation: conditions one and two are cleaner; condition three adds noise.
Include effect size plot in the summary, not just p-values.
State explicitly that the pilot was underpowered.
Consolidated advisor action list: summary, recommendation, battery check, consent forms.

dialogues.speaker: Labmate
dialogues.utterance: Hey, are you using the EEG room tomorrow afternoon?
dialogues.tool: Calendar event — possible EEG room use tomorrow afternoon

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: I need it for participant prep around 1:30, but not the whole afternoon.
dialogues.tool: Calendar event — EEG room prep tomorrow 1:30 PM

dialogues.speaker: Labmate
dialogues.utterance: Could you release the 3 to 5 slot if you’re done? I’m trying to squeeze in a training session.
dialogues.tool: Reminder — release room booking if done early

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: Yes, that’s fine. I’ll know by around 2:30.
dialogues.tool: Reminder — update labmate by 2:30

dialogues.speaker: Labmate
dialogues.utterance: Thanks. Also, we’re almost out of saline gel.
dialogues.tool: Notes — supplies running low

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: Seriously? That explains why the storage box looked sad this morning.
dialogues.tool: None

dialogues.speaker: Labmate
dialogues.utterance: Can you add it to the next order sheet? You were updating the spreadsheet anyway.
dialogues.tool: Reminder — add saline gel to order sheet

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: Yep, I’ll add gel, electrode wipes, and spare caps so we do not repeat this next week.
dialogues.tool: Notes — order sheet items

dialogues.speaker: Labmate
dialogues.utterance: Bless you. Future us appreciates present you.
dialogues.tool: None

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: Future me is already annoyed with present me, but okay.
dialogues.tool: None

dialogues.speaker: Labmate
dialogues.utterance: One more thing — journal club is Friday and you said you might present the memory consolidation paper.
dialogues.tool: Reminder — confirm journal club presentation

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: Right. I should decide tonight so I can actually read it properly.
dialogues.tool: Reminder — decide on Friday journal club paper tonight

dialogues.speaker: Labmate
dialogues.utterance: If you present, I can cover the methods section with you tomorrow morning.
dialogues.tool: Calendar event — optional methods prep tomorrow morning

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: That would help. Let me confirm tonight after I look at the figures.
dialogues.tool: Reminder — text labmate after reviewing figures

dialogues.speaker: Collaborator
dialogues.utterance: Hi Neel, I finally looked at Figure 3. The story is good, but the labeling is doing you no favors.
dialogues.tool: Notes — Figure 3 story is good but labeling is unclear

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: That is the most polite possible way to say my figure is ugly.
dialogues.tool: None

dialogues.speaker: Collaborator
dialogues.utterance: Ugly is fixable. Confusing is the bigger problem.
dialogues.tool: None

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: Fair. Which part was hardest to read?
dialogues.tool: None

dialogues.speaker: Collaborator
dialogues.utterance: The axis labels and the panel titles. Also, the legend assumes readers already know the task design.
dialogues.tool: Notes — figure revision feedback

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: Okay, so I need clearer axes, better panel names, and a legend that restates the task.
dialogues.tool: Notes — figure revision checklist

dialogues.speaker: Collaborator
dialogues.utterance: Exactly. Can you send me a revised version by Monday noon?
dialogues.tool: Reminder — send revised Figure 3 by Monday noon

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: Yes. I should reserve time this weekend because weekday brain is already booked solid.
dialogues.tool: Calendar event — weekend figure revision block

dialogues.speaker: Collaborator
dialogues.utterance: Good plan. Also, the supplementary methods still mention the old exclusion threshold.
dialogues.tool: Reminder — update supplementary methods exclusion threshold

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: Ah, good catch. That would have been embarrassing in circulation.
dialogues.tool: Notes — methods text uses old exclusion threshold

dialogues.speaker: Collaborator
dialogues.utterance: It happens. Better now than after the draft goes wider.
dialogues.tool: None

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: I’ll revise the figure, fix the methods, and send both together Monday.
dialogues.tool: Notes — manuscript revision plan

dialogues.speaker: Landlord
dialogues.utterance: Hi Neel, maintenance can come tomorrow about the kitchen leak. Does 8:30 AM work?
dialogues.tool: Calendar event — plumber visit tomorrow 8:30 AM at apartment

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: Yes, I can be there, but I need them to be on time because I have to leave for campus after that.
dialogues.tool: Reminder — be home for plumber tomorrow morning

dialogues.speaker: Landlord
dialogues.utterance: I’ll tell them to call you when they’re 15 minutes away.
dialogues.tool: None

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: Thanks. The leak under the sink is worse after I run hot water, in case that helps.
dialogues.tool: Notes — kitchen leak details

dialogues.speaker: Landlord
dialogues.utterance: That does help. Please clear the cabinet tonight so they can get in there quickly.
dialogues.tool: Reminder — clear sink cabinet tonight

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: Will do. I’ll move everything out after dinner.
dialogues.tool: Reminder — clear cabinet after dinner

dialogues.speaker: Landlord
dialogues.utterance: If they need a follow-up visit, I’ll message you separately.
dialogues.tool: None

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: Okay. I’d really like to get it fixed this week because the smell is getting annoying.
dialogues.tool: Notes — resident priority: fix leak this week

dialogues.speaker: Mother
dialogues.utterance: Did you eat anything decent today or are you pretending coffee is a food again?
dialogues.tool: None

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: I had yogurt, then lab happened, then time disappeared.
dialogues.tool: None

dialogues.speaker: Mother
dialogues.utterance: That means no. You sound tired.
dialogues.tool: None

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: I am tired, but it’s manageable. I just have a crowded week.
dialogues.tool: None

dialogues.speaker: Mother
dialogues.utterance: Then please at least call your grandmother on Sunday. She keeps asking how your research is going.
dialogues.tool: Reminder — call grandmother Sunday

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: Okay, I can do that Sunday afternoon.
dialogues.tool: Calendar event — call grandmother Sunday afternoon

dialogues.speaker: Mother
dialogues.utterance: And book a haircut. The last photo your sister sent looked like you were being raised by the lab.
dialogues.tool: Reminder — book haircut

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: That is slander, but also fair.
dialogues.tool: None

dialogues.speaker: Mother
dialogues.utterance: I’m serious. You always postpone small things until they become ridiculous.
dialogues.tool: None

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: Fine. Haircut and grandmother call go on the list.
dialogues.tool: Notes — personal upkeep tasks

dialogues.speaker: Friend
dialogues.utterance: A few of us are doing dinner Friday after journal club. Are you coming or are you ghosting us for MATLAB again?
dialogues.tool: Reminder — decide on Friday dinner plans

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: If journal club doesn’t run too late, I’m in.
dialogues.tool: Calendar event — tentative dinner Friday after journal club

dialogues.speaker: Friend
dialogues.utterance: Good. Also, Rahul’s birthday is next week and we’re splitting a gift.
dialogues.tool: Reminder — send share for Rahul’s birthday gift

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: Thanks for reminding me. What’s the damage?
dialogues.tool: None

dialogues.speaker: Friend
dialogues.utterance: Twenty dollars each. I’m collecting by Sunday night.
dialogues.tool: Reminder — send $20 by Sunday night

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: Easy. Ping me the Venmo link because I will absolutely forget otherwise.
dialogues.tool: Reminder — pay when link arrives

dialogues.speaker: Friend
dialogues.utterance: Knew it.
dialogues.tool: None

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: My memory budget is currently funded entirely by external scaffolding.
dialogues.tool: None

dialogues.speaker: Admin
dialogues.utterance: Hi Neel, this is a reminder that your human-subjects refresher training expires next month.
dialogues.tool: Reminder — complete human-subjects refresher training

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: Thanks. Is there a hard deadline before it affects participant scheduling?
dialogues.tool: Notes — payroll deadline may affect participant scheduling

dialogues.speaker: Admin
dialogues.utterance: Yes. If it lapses, you should not run participants until it is renewed.
dialogues.tool: Notes — training lapse blocks participant work

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: Then I should do it this week before I forget and ruin my own schedule.
dialogues.tool: Calendar event — complete refresher training this week

dialogues.speaker: Admin
dialogues.utterance: That would be wise. Also, payroll says your direct deposit form is missing the updated bank attachment.
dialogues.tool: Reminder — upload updated bank attachment for payroll

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: Seriously? I submitted the form, but maybe not the attachment.
dialogues.tool: Reminder — check and upload missing payroll attachment

dialogues.speaker: Admin
dialogues.utterance: Please check today. It is easier to fix before the next stipend cycle closes.
dialogues.tool: Reminder — check payroll portal today

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: Okay. Training and payroll both go on the front page of my brain now.
dialogues.tool: Notes — admin priority items

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: Update the protocol note, do not delete it. The canceled run does not cancel IRB reality.
dialogues.tool: Notes update — update protocol note with consent wording

dialogues.speaker: Research Coordinator
dialogues.utterance: Delete the reminder to print consent forms for tomorrow.
dialogues.tool: Reminder delete — delete consent-form printing reminder for canceled participant

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: Delete it. Printing forms for no one is how labs become paper museums.
dialogues.tool: Reminder delete — remove tomorrow consent forms reminder

dialogues.speaker: Research Coordinator
dialogues.utterance: Maybe the participant reschedules for next week.
dialogues.tool: None

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: Maybe next week is not a calendar event yet.
dialogues.tool: None

dialogues.speaker: Research Coordinator
dialogues.utterance: Set a reminder to follow up if they do not reply by Friday.
dialogues.tool: Reminder — follow up with participant if no reply by Friday

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: Yes, that is specific enough to survive.
dialogues.tool: Reminder — participant follow-up by Friday

dialogues.speaker: Research Coordinator
dialogues.utterance: Can you update the participant reminder to the new time?
dialogues.tool: Clarification - Ask which participant reminder and the exact new time before updating it.

conversations.title: Neel Stateful Lab Scheduling And Protocol Notes

context.why_this_fits: Neel needs precise state for lab blocks, protocol notes, administrative reminders, and ambiguous research schedule edits.

dialogues.speaker: Research Coordinator
dialogues.utterance: EEG room is booked for Monday 2 to 4 for participant N17.
dialogues.tool: Calendar event — EEG room Monday 2-4 for participant N17

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: Block it and remind me Sunday night to print the consent packet.
dialogues.tool: Reminder — print consent packet Sunday night

dialogues.speaker: Research Coordinator
dialogues.utterance: Set a 1:15 alarm Monday for cap setup.
dialogues.tool: Alarm — cap setup Monday 1:15 PM

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: Note that N17 needs the shorter fatigue questionnaire.
dialogues.tool: Notes — participant N17 needs shorter fatigue questionnaire

dialogues.speaker: Research Coordinator
dialogues.utterance: N17 moved to Monday 3 to 5.
dialogues.tool: Calendar event update — move EEG room for N17 to Monday 3-5

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: Update the room block and move the consent reminder to Monday morning.
dialogues.tool: Reminder update — move consent packet reminder to Monday morning

dialogues.speaker: Research Coordinator
dialogues.utterance: Then make the cap setup alarm 2:15.
dialogues.tool: Alarm update — move cap setup alarm to Monday 2:15 PM

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: Update the note: add that N17 prefers breaks after block two.
dialogues.tool: Notes update — add N17 break preference after block two

dialogues.speaker: Research Coordinator
dialogues.utterance: The old IRB upload reminder is obsolete.
dialogues.tool: Reminder delete — remove old IRB upload reminder

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: Delete it, and cancel the practice room block if the pilot is off.
dialogues.tool: Calendar event delete — cancel practice room block if pilot is off

dialogues.speaker: Research Coordinator
dialogues.utterance: There is also an old alarm for a canceled participant setup.
dialogues.tool: Alarm delete — remove old canceled participant setup alarm

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: Delete the note about the outdated electrode map too.
dialogues.tool: Notes delete — remove outdated electrode map note

dialogues.speaker: Research Coordinator
dialogues.utterance: Move that to Wednesday.
dialogues.tool: Clarification — ask which item should move to Wednesday

dialogues.speaker: Neel
dialogues.utterance: Ask which item. In lab-speak, that could be a room, reminder, alarm, or existential crisis.
dialogues.tool: Clarification — ask which item to move before updating
